skill.name: ace-step
skill.description: Generate, inpaint, and outpaint music with ACE Step models on RunComfy using the runcomfy CLI.
skill.summary: Create and edit ACE Step music through RunComfy text-to-audio, inpaint, and outpaint endpoints.
skill.author: runcomfy-com

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns, but review found most high-risk hits are Markdown examples or false positives. The confirmed risks are legitimate RunComfy CLI execution, outbound API calls, user-provided audio URLs, and local token storage, so publication is reasonable with clear user warnings.

medium_findings.title: RunComfy CLI Executes External Jobs
medium_findings.description: The skill authorizes Bash(runcomfy *) and repeatedly instructs the agent to run runcomfy commands. This is expected for the skill, but it executes a third-party CLI that sends job inputs and downloads generated audio.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and command examples directly confirm intended CLI execution. The behavior is legitimate, but it expands the execution and network boundary.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Audio and Prompt Data to RunComfy
medium_findings.description: The skill documents that the CLI posts JSON bodies to the RunComfy Model API and allows source audio URLs for inpaint and outpaint jobs. Prompts, lyrics, tags, and referenced audio may leave the local environment.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file explicitly describes HTTPS audio inputs and API submission. This is clear network data sharing, although limited to the documented service.

low_findings.title: Local RunComfy Token Storage
low_findings.description: The skill states that runcomfy login stores an API token in a hidden configuration file with mode 0600. This is normal credential storage, but users should understand where the token is written and avoid exposing it in prompts or logs.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token path and file mode are documented directly. The risk is limited because the skill advises not to echo or log the token.

low_findings.title: Static High-Risk Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak-cryptography locations do not contain cryptographic operations. They are front matter, music tag examples, prompting guidance, and an exit-code heading, so no weak algorithm use was confirmed.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found no hashing, encryption, or cryptographic API calls. The scanner appears to have matched unrelated prose.

low_findings.title: Static Ruby Backtick Detections Are Markdown Artifacts
low_findings.description: The many Ruby shell backtick findings are inline code spans and fenced command examples in Markdown. They are not Ruby source code and do not create a separate backtick execution vector.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation, not executable Ruby code. Real command risk is already covered by the CLI execution finding.

dangerous_patterns.title: Allowed Bash Access to runcomfy CLI
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants agents permission to call runcomfy commands, which can authenticate, submit model jobs, and write outputs locally.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration and CLI workflow are explicit. The pattern is intended, but it is still security-relevant.

dangerous_patterns.title: Third-Party HTTPS Model API Submission
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill states that the CLI posts job JSON to RunComfy and downloads generated audio into the selected output directory.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network destination and download behavior are documented. No hidden callback or unrelated exfiltration endpoint was found.

content.value_statement: Music teams need fast, low-cost drafts and precise edits without switching tools. This skill routes Claude, Codex, and Claude Code to RunComfy ACE Step generation, inpainting, and outpainting workflows.
actual_capabilities: Runs ACE Step base text-to-audio jobs with tags, lyrics, duration, and seed inputs.
Runs ACE Step 1.5 for multilingual vocal tracks and structured lyrics.
Inpaints a selected time range inside an existing audio file.
Outpaints audio by adding new sections before or after an existing track.
Explains endpoint choice, input fields, pricing, exit codes, and troubleshooting links.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI and a valid RunComfy login or token.
Submits prompts, lyrics, tags, and audio URLs to RunComfy services.
Each generation or edited segment is limited to the documented endpoint duration limits.
The skill does not verify copyright ownership for user-provided lyrics or audio.

use_cases.title: Create draft music libraries
use_cases.description: Generate many low-cost instrumental drafts from genre, mood, instrument, and BPM tags.

use_cases.title: Localize vocal demos
use_cases.description: Use ACE Step 1.5 with structured lyrics to test the same song idea in multiple languages.

use_cases.title: Repair or extend existing tracks
use_cases.description: Replace a weak section with inpainting or add intros and outros with outpainting.

prompt_templates.title: Generate an instrumental draft
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step to create a 60 second instrumental track with lo-fi hip-hop tags, soft drums, rhodes piano, and no vocals.

prompt_templates.title: Create a structured vocal song
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step 1.5 to generate a 90 second indie pop song with verse, chorus, bridge, and outro sections. Include my lyrics and keep the tempo near 120 BPM.

prompt_templates.title: Repair a section with inpainting
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step audio-inpaint on this HTTPS audio URL. Replace seconds 20 through 40 with a softer bridge that matches the original indie pop style.

prompt_templates.title: Extend a hook into an arrangement
prompt_templates.prompt: Use ACE Step audio-outpaint to add a 30 second intro and 60 second outro to this hook. Keep the arrangement energetic and preserve the chorus feel.

output_examples.input: Generate a 60 second mellow instrumental with vinyl texture and soft drums.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects ACE Step base, prepares tag and instrumental lyric inputs, runs the RunComfy job, and returns the saved audio location.

output_examples.input: Replace the last 15 seconds of my uploaded track with a smooth fade.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects audio-inpaint, anchors the edit window to the track end, and requests a soft ambient fade segment.

output_examples.input: Turn this 30 second hook into a two minute track.
output_examples.output: The assistant selects audio-outpaint, adds time before and after the hook, and keeps tags aligned with the original style.

best_practices: Use precise tags for genre, mood, instruments, tempo, and vocal style.
Use ACE Step base for low-cost drafts, then switch to ACE Step 1.5 when vocals need more polish.
Provide only audio URLs and lyrics that you have permission to process.
anti_patterns: Do not submit secret, private, or unreleased audio unless RunComfy processing is acceptable.
Do not use vague tags such as good music when a specific style is needed.
Do not ask the skill to bypass duration, copyright, authentication, or token-handling limits.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates or edits music through ACE Step text-to-audio, audio-inpaint, and audio-outpaint endpoints on RunComfy.

faq.question: Does it need a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: Yes. The RunComfy CLI must be installed, and the user must sign in or provide RUNCOMFY_TOKEN.

faq.question: Can it generate vocals?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports lyrics with section markers, and ACE Step 1.5 is recommended for multilingual vocal work.

faq.question: Can it edit an existing song?
faq.answer: Yes. Inpaint replaces a time range, and outpaint adds audio before or after the source track.

faq.question: Where does my data go?
faq.answer: The CLI sends job inputs to RunComfy services and downloads generated audio to the chosen output directory.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it uses a third-party CLI, network API calls, and local token storage.
